data.bio: Serving as the second Supreme Leader of Iran since 1989. Previously served as President of Iran (1981–1989). Holds ultimate authority over all branches of government and the armed forces. His office oversees foreign policy, nuclear negotiations, and the appointment of key officials.

nodes.label: Ariane Tabatabai
nodes.role: Former Chief of Staff, Office of the Assistant Secretary of Defense

data.role: Former Chief of Staff, Office of the Assistant Secretary of Defense
data.bio: Iranian-American political scientist and former Pentagon official. Served as Chief of Staff in the Office of the Assistant Secretary of Defense for Special Operations and Low-Intensity Conflict. Named in the Semafor investigation as a participant in the Iran Experts Initiative who later gained a senior US government role. Her security clearance was reviewed following the leaked emails.

Former Iranian diplomat and nuclear negotiator. Served as head of the Foreign Relations Committee of Iran's National Security Council. Currently a research scholar at Princeton University's School of Public and International Affairs. Named in leaked emails as a key participant in the Iran Experts Initiative.

Served as the seventh President of Iran from 2013 to 2021. A cleric and lawyer by training, he ran on a platform of diplomatic engagement and economic reform. Oversaw the negotiation and implementation of the JCPOA. Previously served as Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council (1989–2005).

Iranian diplomat who served as Minister of Foreign Affairs from 2013 to 2021 under President Hassan Rouhani. Led the Iranian negotiating team for the JCPOA nuclear deal. Previously served as Iran's Ambassador to the United Nations (2002–2007). Named in the 2023 Semafor investigation into the Iran Experts Initiative.

nodes.label: Saeed Jalili
nodes.role: Former Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council

data.role: Former Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council
data.bio: Iranian politician and diplomat. Served as Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council (2007–2013) and was Iran's chief nuclear negotiator. Ran for president in 2013 and 2024. Known for his hawkish stance on nuclear negotiations and opposition to the JCPOA.

data.description: An informal network established by the Iranian Foreign Ministry to cultivate relationships with academics and policy experts of Iranian heritage in Western countries. Revealed through leaked emails published by Semafor and Iran International in 2023. Participants were provided talking points and guidance aligned with Iranian government positions, particularly around the JCPOA nuclear negotiations.

nodes.label: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Iran

data.name_en: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Iran
data.description: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Islamic Republic of Iran is responsible for the country's foreign policy and diplomatic relations. It manages Iran's embassies and consulates worldwide and leads international negotiations including nuclear diplomacy.

Private research university in Princeton, New Jersey. Its School of Public and International Affairs hosts research scholars working on Middle East policy, including former Iranian diplomats. Relevant to the IEI network as the institutional home of Hossein Mousavian.

data.name: IEI Leaked Emails Published
data.description: Semafor and Iran International published a cache of leaked emails revealing the Iran Experts Initiative, an informal network run by the Iranian Foreign Ministry to cultivate relationships with Western-based academics and policy experts of Iranian heritage. The emails showed participants receiving talking points and guidance from Iranian officials, raising questions about undisclosed foreign government influence on US policy discussions.

sources.title: Iran's foreign minister cultivated a network of US-based academics
Leaked Emails Reveal Iran's Foreign Ministry Ran Influence Network In US
